column2: SQ1: Are you currently responsible for hiring new employees at a U.S.-based company?
column3: Q1: Welcome to Resume Genius's Hiring Managers Survey! We are conducting this research to gain a deeper understanding of how U.S. hiring managers are navigating today’s market. This survey should take less than 5 minutes to complete. Your responses will be kept confidential and used only for research purposes. We appreciate your time and valuable input. To ensure the authenticity and accuracy of this survey’s results, please answer based on your actual experiences and genuine opinions. By submitting the questionnaire, you confirm that all responses are your own truthful views and agree that the information provided may be used for analysis and research purposes.
column4: Q2: What industry best describes your company? (Select the company’s industry, not your job function.)

column6: Q3: In what areas have you or your company implemented AI tools to improve or streamline the recruitment process?_Automating initial candidate outreach and communication
column7: Q3: In what areas have you or your company implemented AI tools to improve or streamline the recruitment process?_Conducting AI-assisted video interviews
column8: Q3: In what areas have you or your company implemented AI tools to improve or streamline the recruitment process?_Matching candidates to job roles based on skills
column9: Q3: In what areas have you or your company implemented AI tools to improve or streamline the recruitment process?_Reducing bias in the recruitment process
column10: Q3: In what areas have you or your company implemented AI tools to improve or streamline the recruitment process?_Writing job descriptions/job postings
column11: Q3: In what areas have you or your company implemented AI tools to improve or streamline the recruitment process?_Scheduling interviews
column12: Q3: In what areas have you or your company implemented AI tools to improve or streamline the recruitment process?_Background checks and verification

column14: Q3: In what areas have you or your company implemented AI tools to improve or streamline the recruitment process?_None of the above
column15: Q4: Which of the following situations have you come across during the recruitment process? (Select all that apply)_Candidates using deepfake technology during video interviews

column17: Q4: Which of the following situations have you come across during the recruitment process? (Select all that apply)_AI-generated user profiles on LinkedIn or other social media profiles
column18: Q4: Which of the following situations have you come across during the recruitment process? (Select all that apply)_AI-generated resumes or cover letters
column19: Q4: Which of the following situations have you come across during the recruitment process? (Select all that apply)_Candidates using AI to cheat on online skills assessments
column20: Q4: Which of the following situations have you come across during the recruitment process? (Select all that apply)_Candidates submitting AI-generated portfolio projects or creative work (e.g., writing samples, art, designs, or code)

column28: Q7: Have you ever hired — or seriously considered — a candidate who reached out through an unconventional channel (e.g., a direct LinkedIn message, social media, or a walk-in)?
column29: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _To build a pipeline of candidates for future roles
column30: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _To gauge salary expectations in the current market
column31: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _To signal growth or stability to investors, clients, or competitors
column32: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _To appear active on job boards for SEO or employer branding reasons
column33: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _To keep current employees motivated or on their toes
column34: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _To satisfy internal HR policies requiring external posting before an internal hire
column35: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _To replace a specific employee who hasn't been let go yet
column36: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _The role was approved but got deprioritized or frozen after posting
column37: Q8: Companies sometimes post job listings for roles they don't actually intend to fill right away — often called "ghost jobs." In your experience, why do companies post job listings like these? _I don't believe companies intentionally post jobs they don't intend to fill
